products.name: Waste Enforcement Network
products.description: The field-deployment and operations product used to detect illegal dumping, qualify incidents, and coordinate fast municipal action on recurring hotspots.

interfaces.name: Edge Device Fleet
interfaces.description: Movable camera and sensor units for priority hotspots.

interfaces.name: Enforcement Operations Console
interfaces.description: Main municipal console for incident review, hotspot planning, and intervention tracking.

interfaces.name: Officer Mobile Workflow
interfaces.description: Field workflow for validation, intervention, and task closure.

jobsToBeDone.name: Reduce repeat dumping on visible municipal hotspots
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Deploy devices quickly, detect actionable incidents, and route the right municipal response before dumping becomes normalized.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Councils reduce incidents and cleanup burden on the sites residents notice most.

steps.description: Choose and activate the right site with low infrastructure overhead.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Deployment Planning and Site Selection
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Prioritizes where to deploy for visible impact.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Edge Camera and Sensor Unit
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Captures field events on the selected hotspot.

steps.description: Filter noise and focus on the incidents most likely to turn into action.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Incident Detection Rules Engine
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Finds suspicious activity on active sites.

top.name: Hotspot effectiveness
top.description: Measures whether instrumented sites show fewer incidents and faster action.

branches.name: Incident reduction
branches.description: Shows whether hotspots actually improve after deployment.

children.name: Confirmed incidents per zone per month
children.description: Lower recurring dumping on active hotspots.

children.name: 30-day hotspot recurrence
children.description: Measure whether the site stays cleaner after intervention.

top.name: Installed-base value proof
top.description: Measures whether each deployment proves budget-worthy value.

branches.name: Municipal ROI
branches.description: Shows whether the program lowers cost and improves public outcomes.

children.name: Cleanup cost avoided
children.description: Estimated cost avoided through faster detection and recurrence reduction.

children.name: Time to first actionable case
children.description: Speed from installation to first meaningful enforcement output.

productStrategy.vision: Make waste enforcement the easiest visible win for local authorities facing recurring dumping hotspots.

1_year.focus: Standardize high-performing waste deployments across the installed base.

customerKPI.northStar: Incident reduction above 50% on mature deployments

businessKPI.northStar: Higher expansion from first deployment to multi-site rollout

3_year.focus: Use the waste network as the platform entry point into broader municipal nuisance response.

customerKPI.northStar: Councils can extend the same operating model into adjacent nuisance categories

businessKPI.northStar: Waste product drives expansion into workflow and analytics modules

milestones: Expansion playbook proven with mature municipalities

5_year.focus: Anchor long-term municipal retention through one field network that supports multiple public-space use cases.

customerKPI.northStar: One municipal field network underpins multiple visible public-space outcomes
supporting: Low operational burden at wider scale

businessKPI.northStar: Field network product remains the durable footprint for platform revenue

neededBricks.whyNeeded: Waste enforcement starts with fast hotspot deployment and credible event capture.

deploymentChannels.deploymentUnit: Movable field unit with secure capture profile
deploymentChannels.pipeline: Configure, deploy, and validate the hotspot installation before live monitoring begins.

neededBricks.whyNeeded: Operators need one place to review incidents, prioritize hotspots, and coordinate municipal action.

deploymentChannels.pipeline: Deploy reviewed workflow changes into the live municipal web console.

products.name: Compliance and Case Management
products.description: The governance and case-core product that turns validated incidents into compliant procedures, notices, audit trails, and treasury-ready enforcement records.

interfaces.name: Municipal Integration API
interfaces.description: Integration surface for partner systems, treasury, and reporting.

jobsToBeDone.name: Prepare defensible municipal enforcement files
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Secure evidence, apply the right procedure, and generate notices or partner handoffs without rebuilding the case manually.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Councils move from alert to sanction faster with less procedural risk.

steps.description: Keep the relevant evidence, access control, and retention state aligned.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Evidence Vault and Chain of Custody
Retention and Deletion Scheduler
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Keeps legal deletion windows under control.

steps.description: Guide case progression according to the relevant legal and administrative path.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Notice and Fine Pack Generator
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Produces the formal notification or sanction package.

top.name: Case defensibility
top.description: Measures whether cases reach sanction or valid referral with low rework.

branches.name: Procedure quality
branches.description: Shows whether the right evidence and legal steps are present.

children.name: Evidence admissibility rate
children.description: Share of reviewed cases that meet evidentiary requirements.

children.name: Cases missing procedural elements
children.description: Cases requiring rework because procedural content is incomplete.

top.name: Workflow leverage
top.description: Measures whether the case core reduces admin work while increasing usable output.

branches.name: Administrative efficiency
branches.description: Tracks whether process automation replaces manual case assembly.

children.name: Officer admin hours per case
children.description: Average administrative effort required per case.

children.name: Partner handoff success rate
children.description: Share of first-pass successful transfers to partner entities.

productStrategy.vision: Make the compliant path the fastest path from incident to enforceable action.

1_year.focus: Standardize case defensibility across waste-enforcement accounts.

3_year.focus: Extend the case core to adjacent offence categories and partner agencies.

customerKPI.northStar: One consistent case model across multiple municipal offence types

5_year.focus: Turn the case core into the procedural backbone of compliant municipal enforcement.

customerKPI.northStar: Councils manage most target offence procedures through one backbone

businessKPI.northStar: High-retention workflow revenue anchored in procedural depth

neededBricks.whyNeeded: The product only works if evidence integrity and procedural workflow are native to the case model.
neededBricks.brickName: Evidence Vault and Chain of Custody

deploymentChannels.pipeline: Apply governance changes and validate access, retention, and case linkage before release.

neededBricks.whyNeeded: Operators and legal teams need a guided case workflow inside the main municipal web surface.

deploymentChannels.pipeline: Promote tested legal workflow updates into the live case workspace.

products.name: Neighborhood Safety Response Pilot
products.description: The adjacent expansion product that reuses the same compliant device, evidence, and workflow foundations for local safety and anti-social behaviour response.

interfaces.name: Enforcement Operations Console
interfaces.description: Shared operator console extended with safety workflows.

interfaces.name: Interagency Evidence Sharing
interfaces.description: Controlled sharing with partner safety and public-order actors.

jobsToBeDone.name: Pilot compliant local safety response without building a second platform
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Reuse field deployments, privacy controls, and case coordination to support selected neighborhood safety incidents.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Municipalities can expand into safety response without multiplying systems or compliance models.

steps.description: Extend the same device and privacy model to selected safety hotspots.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Privacy Zoning and Masking
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Keeps the safety pilot within proportional capture boundaries.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: DPIA and GDPR Compliance Workspace
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides the governance pack needed for safety use cases.

steps.step: Coordinate response with the right partners
steps.description: Use shared evidence and workflow without breaking accountability.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Interagency Evidence Sharing
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Routes the right case material to the right actors.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Enforcement Case Workspace
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Lets municipalities pilot adjacent workflows in the same operating surface.

top.name: Pilot credibility
top.description: Measures whether municipalities trust the safety pilot enough to expand it.

branches.name: Pilot quality
branches.description: Shows whether the shared foundation works for the adjacent use case.

children.name: Pilot incidents resolved with shared workflow
children.description: Safety incidents handled inside the common operating model.

children.name: Compliance exceptions in pilot
children.description: Governance or procedural exceptions raised during pilot use.

top.name: Expansion readiness
top.description: Measures whether the adjacent use case can become a scalable revenue stream.

branches.name: Expansion traction
branches.description: Tracks whether the pilot creates expansion pull in existing accounts.

children.name: Municipalities requesting safety scope expansion
children.description: Existing councils asking to widen the pilot footprint.

children.name: Attach rate of safety module in mature accounts
children.description: Share of mature waste-enforcement accounts adding safety workflows.

productStrategy.vision: Extend the municipal enforcement platform into safety without losing the clarity and restraint that made waste enforcement successful.

1_year.focus: Prove that the shared compliance and case core can support selected safety pilots.

customerKPI.northStar: Pilot incidents resolved with the shared workflow above 70%

businessKPI.northStar: At least 10% of mature accounts request safety expansion

milestones: Shared workflow proven in first accounts

3_year.focus: Productize the strongest repeatable safety workflows.

customerKPI.northStar: Municipalities operate waste and selected safety response in one accountable model

businessKPI.northStar: Safety module becomes a meaningful expansion stream

5_year.focus: Support a broader compliant municipal public-space response platform.

customerKPI.northStar: Councils manage multiple public-space offence classes through one platform

businessKPI.northStar: Expansion revenue from adjacent modules materially increases platform retention

milestones: Common compliance base reused across modules

neededBricks.whyNeeded: The safety pilot can only scale if it reuses the existing compliant deployment and governance foundation.

deploymentChannels.pipeline: Approve the pilot-specific governance package before rollout into pilot municipalities.
